sources.title: Macbeth by William Shakespeare
sources.rightsBasis: Underlying work is public domain in the UK; Project Gutenberg plain-text edition; only short attributed excerpts are retained.

evidence.locator: Act 1, Scene 3; the third witch greets Macbeth
evidence.anchor: All hail, Macbeth! that shalt be king hereafter!

evidence.locator: Act 1, Scene 5; Lady Macbeth tells him to deceive Duncan
evidence.anchor: look like the innocent flower, But be the serpent under’t.

evidence.locator: Act 3, Scene 1; Banquo reflects on Macbeth’s titles
evidence.anchor: Thou hast it now, King, Cawdor, Glamis, all,

evidence.locator: Act 3, Scene 3; Banquo is killed but Fleance escapes
Act 3, Scene 4; Banquo’s ghost takes Macbeth’s place
evidence.anchor: The Ghost of Banquo rises, and sits in Macbeth’s place.

evidence.locator: Act 4, Scene 1; an apparition gives Macbeth confidence
evidence.anchor: none of woman born Shall harm Macbeth.

evidence.locator: Act 1, Scene 4; Macbeth hides his desire
Act 1, Scene 5; Lady Macbeth advises deception
evidence.anchor: look like the innocent flower, But be the serpent under’t.
evidence.requiredAnswer: look like the innocent flower, But be the serpent under’t.

evidence.locator: Act 2, Scene 1; Macbeth sees the dagger
evidence.anchor: Is this a dagger which I see before me,
evidence.requiredAnswer: Is this a dagger which I see before me,

evidence.locator: Act 2, Scene 2; Macbeth looks at his bloody hands
evidence.anchor: Will all great Neptune’s ocean wash this blood
evidence.requiredAnswer: Will all great Neptune’s ocean wash this blood

evidence.anchor: Out, damned spot! out, I say!
evidence.requiredAnswer: Out, damned spot! out, I say!

evidence.locator: Act 5, Scene 5; Macbeth responds to the Queen’s death
evidence.anchor: Life’s but a walking shadow; a poor player,
evidence.requiredAnswer: Life’s but a walking shadow; a poor player,
